mechanism: Zilog Z80 8-bit microprocessor at 6 MHz with 32 KB RAM and 128 KB ROM. A general-purpose stored-program computer -- it genuinely executes arbitrary instructions, just very slowly by modern standards.

historical_notes.claim: The TI-82 uses a Zilog Z80 CPU at 6 MHz with 32 KB RAM (28,734 bytes user-accessible).
Measured active current for the Z80 TI family is ~5-16 mA at ~6 V (peak ~30 mA).

usable_assumptions.confidence: estimate -- Z80 6 MHz / ~10 cycles per instruction (~0.6 MIPS)
